pages.raw_text:
William Quinn, of the machinists'
union, and John Blickendoefer, of the
brewers, have been selected by the
trades council to represent it at the
coming meeting of the State Fedora
ton of Labor in this city in January.
These delegates will be allowed a SAL
airy of s. day for the time they may
spend at the sessions


January II is the date decided upon
for the meeting. Delegates from every
city in the state where there is a union
will be present at the meeting of the
wage workers.


Delegates viii also be present from
Oregon. At the meeting last session in
Seattle over 200 members were present
and it is anticipated the meeting here
will be much larger. Trade unionists
are preparing to entertain the visitors
royalty.
